$schema_note: Testing definition — strategy, tooling, coverage targets, and the key scenarios that must always pass
strategy: Test pyramid: fast unit tests on business rules, integration tests on API+DB behavior (the attendance guarantees live here), a thin E2E layer on the critical user journeys. QR/attendance logic is the highest-risk area and gets the deepest coverage.

tooling.unit: Vitest (api services + shared zod schemas + web hooks/utils)
tooling.component: Vitest + React Testing Library + user-event (web components, forms, guards)
tooling.integration: Vitest + Supertest against the real Express app with a Postgres service container (Testcontainers locally, service container in CI); OSAS mocked with msw/nock fixtures
tooling.e2e: Playwright (chromium + mobile viewport) against a full docker compose stack with seeded data
tooling.static: TypeScript strict, ESLint (typescript-eslint, react-hooks, jsx-a11y), Prettier
tooling.security: npm audit (fail high) + dependency review in CI
tooling.accessibility: axe-core assertions in Playwright on key pages
tooling.load_smoke: k6 script: 100 concurrent scans against one session — p95 < 1s, zero duplicates

coverage_targets.api_integration: Every endpoint: happy path + authz denial + validation failure
coverage_targets.web_components: Shared components and all forms; 70%+ overall
coverage_targets.e2e: The 6 critical journeys below — no percentage goal, but always green

attendance_rules: Scan within grace → PRESENT; after grace → LATE; never scanned + close → ABSENT (BR-03)
Duplicate scan same session → 'already recorded', single row persists (BR-01 unique constraint verified under concurrency: 50 parallel scans, one record)
Scan with expired token window (>±1 of 60s) → TOKEN_EXPIRED_WINDOW
Scan after cutoff time rejected (BR-14); staff manual entry after cutoff allowed + audited
Tampered payload (bad auth tag) → rejected and logged
Static QR session records flagged reduced-security (BR-16)
auth_osas: Active OSAS student logs in → profile upserted, tokens issued
Alumni/inactive OSAS status → 403 STUDENT_INACTIVE, no session (BR-13)
OSAS timeout → OSAS_UNAVAILABLE, no partial account state
Staff lockout after 5 failures; unlock after 15 min
Refresh rotation works; refresh token reuse revokes the whole family
Role escalation attempts (student calling admin endpoints) → 403 for every admin route (matrix sweep test)
scheduling_calendar: Sessions not generated on HOLIDAY/SUSPENSION dates; MAKEUP_CLASS/SPECIAL_EVENT generate extra sessions (BR-15)
Schedule conflict rejected (same section, faculty, or room overlap)
OSAS sync upsert preserves local-only fields (adviser) and logs results
corrections_and_overrides: Override without reason → VALIDATION_FAILED; with reason → audited with before/after
Correction approve updates record with marked_by=CORRECTION; reject leaves record untouched; student notified in both cases
reports: Rates computed from raw records match hand-computed fixtures (daily/weekly/monthly/semester)
At-risk detection: exactly 3 consecutive absences triggers; 2 does not (BR-07)
CSV/XLSX/PDF exports open and contain expected rows
e2e_journeys: Student: login (mock OSAS) → dashboard → scan seeded session QR → sees PRESENT confirmation → record visible in My Attendance
Faculty: login → start session → live QR rotates → simulated scans appear in roster live → close → absents auto-marked → session report correct
Faculty: manual attendance + override with reason → audit entry visible to admin
Student files correction → faculty approves → status and notification updated
Admin: create subject→section→offering→schedule→enroll section → sessions pre-generated next day (clock mocked)
Admin: run department report and export XLSX

test_data.seeds: Deterministic factory fixtures (faker with fixed seed): 1 term, 2 courses, 4 sections, 6 subjects, 3 faculty, 60 students, 8 offerings with schedules
test_data.osas_mock: Fixture server with personas: active student, alumni, inactive, timeout, malformed-response
test_data.time_control: All time-dependent tests use injected clock (no sleeps); token windows tested at boundaries

ci_integration: PR: static + unit + integration (must pass to merge). main: + E2E + build. Nightly: full suite + k6 smoke + npm audit report.
quality_gates: New business rules require tests in the same PR
Bug fixes require a regression test
Flaky tests quarantined within 24h and fixed within a week
